# Copilot Instructions

## Project Guidelines
- iOS and watchOS app icons are tracked directly in each target's `Assets.xcassets/AppIcon.appiconset/` so the project builds out-of-the-box without copy steps. Keep `ASSETCATALOG_COMPILER_APPICON_NAME = AppIcon` enabled for iOS target configs so `CFBundleIconName` is generated. The project is designed to work out-of-the-box without special manual copy flows, repo instruction-file dependencies, or separate workaround flows for app icons.
- When building the iOS app locally, must create `ios/Local.xcconfig` from `ios/Local.xcconfig.template` and set `DRIZZLE_DEVELOPMENT_TEAM` to the Apple Developer Team ID. The file is gitignored so the Team ID is never committed. The watchOS app reuses the same `ios/Local.xcconfig` and must also ensure that the watchOS app remains an independent watch-only app behavior (WKWatchOnly).
- When bumping the version for a new release, always update `versionName` in `android/app/build.gradle.kts` to match the new version number. The Android workflow reads this value to auto-rename the APK/AAB to `Drizzle_v{version}`.
- For Play Store Android releases, always build a signed AAB (`bundleRelease`) and keep signing secrets in gitignored `android/keystore.properties` or GitHub Actions secrets, never in tracked files.
- When bumping the version for a new release, also update `CFBundleShortVersionString` in `ios/iDrizzle/Info.plist` to match the new version number.
- When bumping the version for a new release, also update `CFBundleShortVersionString` in `ios/iDrizzleWatch Watch App/Info.plist` to match the new version number (keep it aligned with the iOS app).
- When bumping the version for a new release, also update the Windows `VERSIONINFO` block in `WeatherGlance.rc` (`FILEVERSION`, `PRODUCTVERSION`, and the `FileVersion`/`ProductVersion` strings) to match. The unsigned `Drizzle.exe` keeps the same filename across builds, so an embedded version is required to avoid SmartScreen/AV "same name, different binary" vulnerability flags. Set `FILEVERSION`/`PRODUCTVERSION` to `MAJOR,MINOR,PATCH,N` where `N` is the sequential build count.
- Build-number scheme: the Xcode build field (`CFBundleVersion` in both `ios/iDrizzle/Info.plist` and `ios/iDrizzleWatch Watch App/Info.plist`) uses the decimal form `N.WAIS`. `N` is the sequential build count. The four fraction digits are per-platform "modified in this build" flags in fixed order Windows, Android, iOS, watchOS (`1` = changed, `0` = unchanged). Example: `2.1111` = build 2 touching all platforms; `3.0001` = build 3 where only watchOS changed. When making a new build, increment `N` and set the flags to reflect exactly which platforms were modified. Android's `versionCode` must be an integer, so it tracks only the sequential build count `N` (keep it in sync with the `N` part of `CFBundleVersion`). NOTE: App Store Connect parses `CFBundleVersion` as dot-separated integers and strips leading zeros, so `3.0011` is stored/compared as `3.11` and `3.0001` as `3.1`. Uniqueness/ordering is therefore driven by `N` always increasing — always bump `N` for every upload (never reuse an `N`, even if only re-uploading the same platform set).

## Toolchain Currency
- Keep all build toolchains and dependencies on current or latest-LTS versions (Android SDK/AGP/Gradle/Kotlin, Xcode/iOS/watchOS deployment targets, Node, and web/CDN libraries). Do not build against old/EOL toolchains.
- Java is the exception: pin the build JVM to Java 21 LTS (do not move to newer non-LTS or newer LTS JDKs without an explicit request). The Android Java toolchain tracks Java 21 rather than Java 17.
- Do not downgrade the watchOS/iOS deployment targets to silence warnings; keep them on the latest supported targets (watchOS 26.5).

## Release Management
- For each version release, explicitly create GitHub Releases for version tags and include built Windows assets (Drizzle.exe and a zip, versioned like `Drizzle_vX.Y.Z.zip`).
- Adopt proactive release workflows to ensure future-proofing, including early migration for platform deprecations (e.g., opt into Node 24 ahead of enforced rollout).
